headline: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close
summary: Asian tech stocks sank again Friday as the rollercoaster ride that has characterised the week continued into the weekend, while crude prices edged back down after a brief rally sparked by news of an attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.Oil prices resumed the downward shift that has marked the week, having risen around two percent Thursday on news that a cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast in Hormuz.

content.title: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close
content.summary: Asian tech stocks sank again Friday as the rollercoaster ride that has characterised the week continued into the weekend, while crude prices edged back down after a brief rally sparked by news of an attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.Oil prices resumed the downward shift that has marked the week, having risen around two percent Thursday on news that a cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast in Hormuz.

headline: World shares skid as traders sell to lock in profits after recent rallies driven by AI
summary: World shares retreated Friday, led by heavy losses in Japan and South Korea as traders sold to lock in gains from recent rallies in stocks related to artificial intelligence.  Britain's FTSE 100 shed 0.4% to 10,490.62.  The wide swings in Tokyo and Seoul are typical of recent volatility in markets as investors react to the deluge of dollars heading into AI data centers  and other investments.

content.title: World shares skid as traders sell to lock in profits after recent rallies driven by AI
content.summary: World shares retreated Friday, led by heavy losses in Japan and South Korea as traders sold to lock in gains from recent rallies in stocks related to artificial intelligence.  Britain's FTSE 100 shed 0.4% to 10,490.62.  The wide swings in Tokyo and Seoul are typical of recent volatility in markets as investors react to the deluge of dollars heading into AI data centers  and other investments.

thumbnail.caption: A currency trader passes by a screen showing the Korea Composite Stock Price Index (KOSPI) and the foreign exchange rate between U.S. dollar and South Korean won at the foreign exchange dealing room of the Hana Bank headquarters in Seoul, South Korea, Friday, June 26, 2026. headline: Stock Futures Slump as Tech Selloff Rages On After Mag 7 Dumped
summary: Tech stocks were on track to extend their recent slump on Friday as investors continued to fret about soaring memory-chip costs and wondered if the AI trading frenzy may be about to fizzle out.  Nasdaq 100 futures dropped 1.1%.  The Nasdaq closed in the red on Thursday as investors ditched the Magnificent Seven group of mega-cap tech stocks.

content.title: Stock Futures Slump as Tech Selloff Rages On After Mag 7 Dumped
content.summary: Tech stocks were on track to extend their recent slump on Friday as investors continued to fret about soaring memory-chip costs and wondered if the AI trading frenzy may be about to fizzle out.  Nasdaq 100 futures dropped 1.1%.  The Nasdaq closed in the red on Thursday as investors ditched the Magnificent Seven group of mega-cap tech stocks.

headline: U.S. chip stocks retreat after Apple price hikes stoke fears over AI trade
summary: Investing.com -- U.S. semiconductor stocks slipped in premarket trading on Friday, extending a global technology selloff after Apple raised prices on several of its products, fanning concerns that surging memory chip costs could dampen consumer electronics demand and cloud the outlook for the broader AI trade.

headline: European Chip Stocks Fall as AI Selloff Resumes
summary: Shares of European semiconductor companies were following their Asian peers in negative territory as investors ditched stocks linked to artificial intelligence. A strong set of earnings and outlook from Micron Technology late Wednesday failed to reignite a rally in AI stocks on Thursday.

content.title: European Chip Stocks Fall as AI Selloff Resumes
content.summary: Shares of European semiconductor companies were following their Asian peers in negative territory as investors ditched stocks linked to artificial intelligence. A strong set of earnings and outlook from Micron Technology late Wednesday failed to reignite a rally in AI stocks on Thursday.

headline: US Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Futures Rise As Tech Leads Inflation Watch
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Friday, Jun, 26 2026 US stock futures are pointing higher this morning, with E-mini S&P 500 contracts up about 0.7% and Nasdaq-100 futures ahead more than 2%, as investors focus on three big signals. First, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, PCE for May, is expected to run hotter, which simply means the cost of living may not be easing as quickly as households would like and could keep interest rates higher for longer. Second, US oil inventories...
